volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Trojan Women (Section 1280 - Section 1.1331)

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: δούλας · ἰὼ θεοί . καὶ τί τοὺς θεοὺς καλῶ ;
sections.translation: to slavery . O gods ! Yet why do I call on the gods ? They did not hearken ever before to our call . Come , let us rush into the flames , for to die with my country in its blazing ruin would be a noble death for me .

sections.label: Section 1284
sections.translation: Your sorrows drive you frantic , poor lady .

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἄγετε , μὴ φείδεσθʼ · Ὀδυσσέως δὲ χρὴ
sections.translation: Go , lead her away , make no delay , for you must deliver her into the hand of Odysseus , conveying to him his prize .

sections.label: Section 1287
sections.translation: Woe ! oh woe ! Son of Cronos , prince of Phrygia , father of our race ,

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.translation: do you behold our sufferings now , unworthy of the stock of Dardanus ?

sections.label: Section 1291
sections.translation: He sees them , but our mighty city is a city no more , and Troy ’ s day is done .

sections.label: Section 1294
sections.translation: Woe ! oh woe !

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.translation: Ilium is ablaze ; the homes of Pergamos and its towering walls are now one sheet of flame .

sections.label: Section 1298
sections.original: πτέρυγι δὲ καπνὸς ὥς τις οὐ-
sections.translation: As the smoke soars on wings to heaven , so sinks our city to the ground before the spear .

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.translation: With furious haste both fire and enemy spear devour each house .

sections.label: Section 1.1302
sections.original: ἰὼ γᾶ τρόφιμε τῶν ἐμῶν τέκνων .
sections.translation: Oh , earth , nourisher of my children !

sections.label: Section 1.1303
sections.translation: Ah , ah !

sections.label: Section 1.1303a
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα , κλύετε , μάθετε ματρὸς αὐδάν .
sections.translation: Hearken , my children , hear your mother ’ s voice .

sections.label: Section 1.1304
sections.translation: You are calling on the dead with voice of lamentation .

sections.label: Section 1.1305
sections.original: γεραιά γʼ ἐς πέδον τιθεῖσα μέλεα καὶ
sections.translation: Yes , as I stretch my aged limbs upon the ground , and beat upon the earth with both my hands .

sections.label: Section 1.1307
sections.translation: I follow you and kneel , invoking from the nether world my hapless husband .

sections.label: Section 1.1310
sections.translation: I am being dragged and hurried away —

sections.label: Section 1.1310b
sections.translation: The sorrow , the sorrow of that cry !

sections.label: Section 1.1311
sections.translation: To dwell beneath a master ’ s roof !

sections.label: Section 1.1311b
sections.translation: From my own country !

sections.label: Section 1.1312
sections.translation: Woe is me ! O Priam , Priam , slain , unburied , left without a friend , nothing do you know of my cruel fate .

sections.label: Section 1.1315
sections.translation: No , for over his eyes black death has drawn his pall , a pure man slain by the impure .

sections.label: Section 1.1317
sections.original: ἰὼ θεῶν μέλαθρα καὶ πόλις φίλα ,
sections.translation: Woe for the temples of the gods and for our dear city !

sections.label: Section 1.1317a
sections.translation: Ah , ah !

sections.label: Section 1.1318
sections.original: τὰν φόνιον ἔχετε φλόγα δορός τε λόγχαν .
sections.translation: Murderous flame and enemy spear are now your lot .

sections.label: Section 1.1319
sections.original: τάχʼ ἐς φίλαν γᾶν πεσεῖσθʼ ἀνώνυμοι .
sections.translation: Soon will you tumble to your own loved soil , and be forgotten .

sections.label: Section 1.1320
sections.original: κόνις δʼ ἴσα καπνῷ πτέρυγι πρὸς αἰθέρα
sections.translation: And the dust , mounting to heaven on wings like smoke , will rob me of the sight of my home .

sections.label: Section 1.1322
sections.original: ὄνομα δὲ γᾶς ἀφανὲς εἶσιν · ἄλλᾳ δʼ
sections.translation: The name of my country wiII pass into obscurity ; all is scattered far and wide , and hapless Troy has ceased to be .

sections.label: Section 1.1325
sections.translation: Did you know , did you hear ?

sections.label: Section 1.1325b
sections.translation: Yes , it was the crash of the citadel .

sections.label: Section 1.1326
sections.translation: The shock , the shock —

sections.label: Section 1.1326b
sections.translation: Will overwhelm our city utterly .

sections.label: Section 1.1327
sections.translation: O woe is me ! trembling , quaking limbs , support my footsteps ! away ! to face

sections.label: Section 1.1330
sections.translation: the day that begins your slavery .

sections.label: Section 1.1331
sections.translation: Woe for our unhappy town ! And yet let us advance to the Achaean fleet .
